summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents with dry-run default, explicit authorization requirements, and git-backed file snapshot verification.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots implement file-level boundary audit using git ls-files and filesystem walks
evidence.why: Core boundary enforcement mechanism tracks project file mutations before/after agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary audit runs after agent execution, comparing before/after snapshots to detect violations
evidence.why: Post-execution boundary check ensures agents did not mutate files outside allowed roots

evidence.summary: execution_policy.default_mode is 'dry_run' requiring explicit executeAgents=true and allowAgentDispatch=true flags
evidence.why: Default safety prevents accidental agent execution without user authorization

evidence.summary: Execute mode requires both executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch flags to be true
evidence.why: Dual-flag requirement ensures intentional authorization for agent dispatch

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers are added to readiness when boundaryChanges are detected
evidence.why: Boundary violations block PRD readiness even when agent results pass

evidence.summary: gitFiles function uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' for git-backed boundary snapshots
evidence.why: Git integration detects untracked and modified project files during boundary audit

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates that evidence records declare scope as project/external/user/unknown
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation prevents assumptions from being promoted to project facts

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines required evidence structure with scope validation
evidence.why: Schema contract enforces evidence discipline and scope separation

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues checks that project facts include concrete file locators
evidence.why: Project evidence without paths blocks readiness to prevent vague claims

evidence.summary: Test suite verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test coverage confirms boundary enforcement overrides successful agent results

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary tests detect untracked and gitignored project mutations
evidence.why: Git integration tests verify boundary audit catches all file changes

evidence.summary: External-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts and project evidence requires concrete locator
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation tests ensure project facts require project-scoped evidence

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation creates claude -p invocations with permission-mode, settings, and tool restriction flags
evidence.why: Provider adapter configures tool boundaries for evidence agents

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent instructions with boundary rules and evidence schema
evidence.why: Agent prompt enforces write boundary and evidence structure requirements

risks: If boundary audit has filesystem race conditions or git state timing issues, violations could escape detection
If provider adapter tool configuration fails to enforce boundaries, agents could mutate project files despite audit

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is complete with git-backed snapshots, dry-run defaults, dual-flag authorization, scope validation, and comprehensive test coverage. Evidence dispatch correctly blocks project mutations and requires explicit authorization.
